title: SQL: foreign keys without not-null constraint -> meltdown
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50));
CREATE TABLE t2 (id INT PRIMARY KEY, t1_id INT, CONSTRAINT fk_t1 FOREIGN KEY (t1_id) REFERENCES t1(id));
INSERT INTO t2 (id, t1_id) VALUES (1, NULL);
SELECT * FROM t2 WHERE t1_id IS NULL;
